violations.text: Food debris and bags of trash observed outside of the dumpster. Employee picked up trash and put it into dumpster during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026
Bag of trash observed on the ground beside the dumpster. Establishment had an employee clean up the trash and put it in the dumpster. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by many crows observed in and around the dumpsters. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment cleaned all trash from around the dumpsters to remove attractants and harborage conditions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

The refuse area observed with a large amount of trash and boxes overflowing the dumpsters and surrounding the dumpsters. Establishment had employees clean trash from around the dumpsters so that Waste Management could empty the dumpster. New dumpsters were delivered to the site during inspection. The new dumpsters were then filled with surrounding trash for pick-up tomorrow. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

A large amount of litter and trash observed in the dumpster area. Establishments and its premises shall be kept free of litter and trash to prevent harborage conditions. Establishment cleaned up dumpster area during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

The dumpster outside was observed with an excess of litter, trash, and cardboard. Refuse areas must be maintained. Corrective Action - Establishment is working to clean area of refuse. Establishment is calling Dumpster company for an additional pick up today. Establishment is to work to get daily dumpster service. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment has employees certified through an approved food safety management course. One employee must register with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager. Application provided. New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knife) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

Beans were observed held at 124- qnd 101- degrees Fahrenheit in hot hold units. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Beans were reheated to 177- and 193-degrees F during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

Steak was observed held at 113 degrees Fahrenheit at the service line. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Steak was reheated to above 165 degrees F and returned to hot hold unit during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

The door to the outside, located in receiving, is not tight-fitting. Space observed at the bottom of the door (right side). Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/13/2024

Refrigerator door gasket of the walk-in cooler observed in poor repair, split away from the door. Per interview of manager, establishment has a new gasket. Gasket is to be installed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/13/2024

The door to the outside, located in the receiving door (rear), is not tight-fitting. Small gaps observed on both sides (bottom). Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2023

The door to the outside, located in the warewashing area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2023
